channel.title: /biz/ Active (filtered, with replies)
channel.description: Filtered out: general, gme, bbbyq. OP + first 3 replies + last 30 replies.

item.title: Redpill me on RV life — 23 replies — 92.0/hr
item.content:encoded:
Redpill me on RV life

Open thread • Replies: 23 • Updated: 2026-03-19 19:51 UTC

OP

Assume I wouldn't even have to drive very much, just around town randomly.

Early replies (first 3)

62003573
RV feels overkill, I think van life if you're single is better

62003620
trashy neighbors, lot fees are super gay, high maintenance, burn a lot of gas, easy to rob, difficult to sell, buying a used one is gross, and they depreciate quickly when you buy them new

62003633
>>62003573
Van seems like an overkill. I think sedan life if you're single is better.

Latest replies (last 30, oldest → newest)

62003773
>>62003565
If you deal from office like most people here you're supposed to get proper rest in a proper house. Like what the other goy posted, you're going to have to pay for lot fees after you purchased used RV in decent condition (incredibly rare) upfront.

RV might be worth it if you're a nomadic dealer that can do your dealings from and at anywhere in the real world though.. preferably in North America or Central Asia. So it depends entirely on the goyim, what kind of goy are you?

62003777
>>62003633
Sedan seems like an overkill. I think cardboard box life if you're single is better.

62003892
>>62003565
It’s good, you can finance a good brand one for 10 or 15 years, and make your payments like $400 or less. You can also find a lot to rent for around the same cost as your RV payment, sometimes utilities are even included

62003922
>>62003565
A RV like that costs as much as a small house, your reality will more look like what you call a trailer park

Youre maybe not allowed to park them anywhere you want or even in town

62004450
>>62003565
It only works if you are a traveling tradie that can secure work at a jobs site for months on end and stay at a camp ground. I see this with the data centers they are building in my area. Some of the electricians will have a 5th wheel trailer and use the truck to commute to and from the campground.

62004695
>>62003565
how is an rv good? just have a trailer rvs fucking suck

maybe even build it yourself since people try to scam you by marking them up too much

62004702
>>62004450
Make pallet camper and you can go anywhere

https://www.youtube.com/watch?v=QpO63kVjFb0

62004717
It's neither here nor there.
If you want something that big get a trailer or small house.
A van is a better idea, ideally a stealth one that isn't obvious you are living in it.
An RV or van that is clearly your home screams PLEASE COME HARASS AND/OR ROB ME.

62004741
Shit insulation if you live in the cold

62004743
>>62003565
The trick is to buy some cheap strip of land then get a used 30k camper trailer and put it on it. Boom, you've got a house with no monthly payments and all you have to do is pay taxes on the land every year.
The important part is to look very hard for a good deal. Someone I know got a very nice one, basically as large as a camper trailer can go, for 30k from a couple who didn't want it anymore. Never, ever buy from dealerships or buy new, they will vacuum money right out of your wallet.

62004847
>>62004695
You need one of them RVs that you can rig to the back of a pickup truck and not take up extra space beyond the truck's original space. There was a guy that lived in one of these in a parking lot for two years where I'm at.

62004973
>>62004847
that doesn’t seem like an improvement over living in a van

62005068
>>62003620
You act like this asshole isn't just going to park in front of random houses and industrial zone "no mans land".

62005158
>>62004450
this. some of the guys throwing up solar farms in western PA lived like this to save a portion of their per diem. might be worth it if you can secure a decent spot at the right price.

62005211
>>62003777
An apartment seems excessive to me; a tent near a sewer and a place where syringes can be found seems more suitable for a single person.

62006081
>>62003565
It can work in certain situations. Most of the time it's just better to rent with roommates or live with your parents. There are tons of youtubers living in vans, RVs, and even regular cars, some in cold climates.
>>62004741
You can always wear more layers for the cold. There are people who can sleep in tents in the Canadian woods during a snow. The real problem is heat. Assuming you can just spend time somewhere else during the day, if the night is above 30C good luck falling asleep. I once spent one summer without AC in a place where it's 43C during the day and around 28C-30C during the night. I was lucky enough to live on the ground floor so I was quite insulated during the day, and during the night I had to keep the main door open and only have a net aluminium door. I used to keep dozens of full water bottles so they could absorb heat during the day and radiate it out during the night using a fan. Shit like this is not feasible in a van. You will need a power source and use an AC.

62006094
>>62003565
You may as well just get a condo. At least it'll increase in value.

62006433
>>62006081

Yeah, people can technically not die in cold. You are 100% correct. It's not really a "gotcha!" moment if you wear 4 layers not to die when you exit your home when peopl in California literally never have to worry about their clothing. Still there is absolutely nothing you can do in -3 fahrenheit. You can walk from your house to a heated building. Without massive infrastructure people are just dead. You can enjoy a beverage outside at 95 fahrenheit. Heck, or just lie outside in the sun

t. finnish turan

62006466
>>62003565
The worst thing about living in an RV is where you have to shit. Unless you have a proper toilet you're going to hate whatever solution you get. You either shitting in a box you have to then empty by hand or you're shitting in a mini toilet that flushes into a tank under your feet that you have to empty or you're shitting into some weird variation with wood chips or blue Porto Potty water.

62006502
>>62004743
You have to be careful with local laws if you do this. A lot of places won't let you live like that. Helps if you can use a family member's place for your home address.

item.title: At this point I don't think there's going to be a crash I think there's going to be a slow bleed until MAGS is at $15 wh… — 2 replies — 8.0/hr
item.content:encoded:
At this point I don't think there's going to be a crash I think there's going to be a slow bleed until MAGS is at $15 wh…

Open thread • Replies: 2 • Updated: 2026-03-19 19:51 UTC

OP

At this point I don't think there's going to be a crash I think there's going to be a slow bleed until MAGS is at $15 where it'll flatten out

Early replies (first 3)

62006476
>>62005823
Agree fully. I'm hedging against growth. It just makes no sense. If youd look at the S&P493 youd see that MAG7 had fully inflated everything and their entire spending is goign towards AI, which hasnt really created any value yet.

62006505
>>62005823
>>62006476
Cool.

Latest replies (last 30, oldest → newest)

No additional replies in the latest window.

item.title: YLYL — 115 replies — 97.0/hr
item.content:encoded:
YLYL

Open thread • Replies: 115 • Updated: 2026-03-19 18:50 UTC

OP

Early replies (first 3)

61970837
>>61970830
FUCK LOST ON FPBP

61970841
>>61970825

Latest replies (last 30, oldest → newest)

61996186
>>61995965
>trying this hard

61996551
>>61970837
i lost hard, even though i knew what was gonna happen, that was great.

61996569
>>61973123
please be real, got a link?

61996575
>>61994101
its a holdover thing from poor working class boomers, my grandparents had a similar photo with their second last car they ever bought. They weren't retards like this though, they bought a sensible car for a sensible price.

61996581
>>61996569
https://3dvf.com/en/they-spent-e1300-on-electricity-in-an-airbnb-the-owner-discovers-they-were-mining-cryptocurrencies/

61996602
>>61995758
>$250,000 loan
I’ll have more saved up by that age, that’s a drop in a bucket for a billionaire lol

61996607
>>61994075
they're niggers, they're gonna make 1-2 payments then close the account it withdraws from, it doesnt matter how much they agree to pay, the banks deal with the bullshit loans, the car yards already made their money.

This shit should be banned, because it results in white taxpayers having to foot the bill eventually.

>>61996581
no anon, i meant the image, of course some sperg would take their mining rig with them while on holiday and just plug it in.

61996696
>>61994906
i've heard about this lmao. This is the sort of crime i approve of, if your company is dumb enough to pay this, fuck em.

61996722
no clue why people hate on J$
he's the literal embodiment of the judeo-american dream of being a rich pimp

61997670
>>61996722
lost again. well done bro. well done.

61997793
>>61970841
that was the best fucking thread of all time

61997820
>>61997816
worth the ban & fuck jannies they do it for free!!!

61998617
>>61970888
This will never stop beeing hilarious

61998894
>>61997816
>a 2nd sharpie has hit the linkie anus
I missed this one. We did get one to do it earlier. I wonder how many of them have done this?

61998962
>>61998894
As the cuckolds of crypto is probably all of them

61999010
>>61979728
Cant tell me these are ragebait by now, the first ones surely were real but 427 months? Cmon

61999152
I wish i could see again the thread of the anon Who got delivered a tonshit of wood beacuse his futures expired or some shit kek

62000194
>>61999152
I saw this posted recently

62000503
>>61999152
Is this the new mandela effect patch?
I swear it was oil barrels years ago

62001140
>>61999152
it was lumbers futures: >> 61970841

62001144
>>61999152

see ffs >>61970841

62003933
https://youtu.be/AfnBcOwX0Yo?si=5YrANTCU-oIqP2Zl

62004213
>>61976200
Black people can't into Maffmatikz...

62004292
>>62003933
Lost again

item.title: LINK is starting its hyper super cycle once in a lifetime run. — 74 replies — 55.2/hr
item.content:encoded:
LINK is starting its hyper super cycle once in a lifetime run.

Open thread • Replies: 74 • Updated: 2026-03-19 18:41 UTC

OP

LINK is starting its hyper super cycle once in a lifetime run. Hope your bags are packed. This is unironically the moment we've been waiting for.

Early replies (first 3)

61990544
Are you saying artemis wasn't a larp

61990577
>>61990540
It’s gonna dump soon. We have been doing this dance for almost a decade now. Sadly there is not much to expect until we have actual real life adoption.

61990580
>>61990540
Its 5% bro, wake me when we pass $30

Insider trading on the clarity act is unlikely

Latest replies (last 30, oldest → newest)

61994019
>>61993046
Artemis you have been the lamest larper

61994053
>>61993046
kys

61994074
https://www.youtube.com/watch?v=BLs_NP0A1Vw

we are gonna make it

61994082
I boughteded more frens
Missed february cheapies because my truck turned gay but we're back

61994241
>>61993517
>aroha

61994249
>>61990540
XRP is mogging us, it’s up 10% we are only up 4%. By the way 10% for XRP is ~10 billion in increased mcap more than chainlinks market cap….

61994261
>>61990544
Can someone share or link his prediction I need some copium rn

61994286
>>61994261

61994326
>>61994286

61994330
>>61994326
Where’s the March prediction

61994642
YOU GOTTA FIGHT
FOR YOUR RIGHT
TO HATE LIIIIINKIIIIEES

61994646
>>61993046

an hero NOW

61994741
>>61990540
I don't even care if I make it I just want to see my GME baggie friends cope and seethe by glorious LINK price action

61994752
>>61993046
Since you're here, please tell me... are they planning to implement a "whitelist" for stakers? Like, only approved entities can stake? It's been my deepest fear about the delays to staking.

61994758
>>61994752
of course. what... you thought.. LMAO.. you were gonna get to stake? with the big boys? you stupid neet ass faggot LMAO ROFL LMAO

61995921
>>61993046
I predict: -2.4%

61995937
>>61990540
This year for sure

61996432
just got to 14k linkies, keep stackin

61997084
I AM SORRY FOR DOUBTING I KNEEL
https://x.com/chainlink/status/2034020963952869473

61997164
>>61993046
was the sec news what you were referring to or something bigger?? how important was it for clarity that link is a digital commodity?

61998976
>>61991990
the profoundness of this post

61999433
>duuuuuuuuuuuuuude i'm in a secret!!!!

oh yeah?

>yeah duuuuuuuuuuude i i i'm early!!!

so you're early to an altcoin that went 200x 6 years ago? are you sure?

>duuuuuuuuuuuude y y yer an xrp fudder

why did you bring up xrp

>duuuuuuuuuuuuuude you jack off!!!

umm ok this is getting weird

61999453
I just read the whitepaper and I didn't see anything about the tokin being necessary.

62002606
>>61999433
pool's still closed dude

62003757
I've been here for about 10 years and only scroll without posting but I must inquire, what is wrong with you people? Is there a reason behind this irrational obsession with a coin that's been dead for years? There's absolutely no way in hell someone has been paid for 10 years to flood the board with chainlink spam.

Is this some profound ironic doomkino comedy only for those with an exquisite palate?

62003759
>>62003757
Peel

62003788
>>62003759
these posts will continue until morale improves

62003864
>>62003757
This guy has a diaper fetish...he probably pissed his adult diaper while typing this subtle fud attempt

62006127
>>62003757
Dude, they are shills.
Link is just the personal fundraiser/patreon of sergey, is a scam.

item.title: Still Selling BTC? Big Mistake — 41 replies — 53.9/hr
item.content:encoded:
Still Selling BTC? Big Mistake

Open thread • Replies: 41 • Updated: 2026-03-19 19:16 UTC

OP

I’ve been telling folks here not to sell BTC and now they’re buying at the top.

>When are you gonna learn your lesson, folks? HODL

Early replies (first 3)

61996853
>>61996755
rich kid but black

61996883
>>61996853
What's the problem?

61998635
>>61996755
Sell and wait for 10k per btc

Latest replies (last 30, oldest → newest)

61999382
>>61996853
It doesn't matter

61999808
>>61998813
Until you cant afford

62001975
>>61996883
I'm also wondering

62003031
>>61998667
Chill, BTC isn’t dumping unless something crazy happens. Just hold.

62003045
Dump? Maybe, maybe not. Either way, HODL or stack more if you can.

62003053
niggers

62003063
>>61998635
I'm not selling

62003070
>>62003031
>>62003045
I’m not just holding my BTC, I’m staking it natively on Babylon. Way smarter than just holding for no return.

62003088
>>62003070
Is that possible? I'm afraid of those yield offerings

62003094
>>62003053
hello nigger

62003101
>>62003070
>>62003088
sirs?

62003111
>>61996755
If you’re in for the long term, short-term dips aren’t worth worrying about. Just stay in control and HODL.

62003117
BTC might retrace a bit, but don’t panic sell. Plan your moves, don’t let fear drive trades.

62003136
>>62003088
It's 0.07% APR. And you will be sending your BTC to an anonymous system whose owner pinky promises to send you your money back if you ask nicely.

62003422
>>62003088
It’s possible, just look into it yourself. you’ll get it. Self custody is the advantage.

62003430
>>61996755
Let the goyim deal in the crypto world.

62003444
>>62003136
“Anonymous system” lol. It’s literally built around cryptographic guarantees, not trust. APR is low, sure, but better than nothing. This isn’t sending BTC to some random yield farm.

62003448
>>61998652
He's a great kiddo

62003660
>>62003430
Bitcoin keeps improving. Lightning, Taproot, and other upgrades make BTC faster, cheaper, and more usable, confidence grows, price follows.

62003668
Market sentiment drives crypto hard. A little media buzz, some whales buying, and suddenly BTC is pumping. FOMO is real.

62003679
>>62003444
The only defi right now is ethereum

62003695
>>62003117
Whats your price target?

62003910
>>62003679
“Only Ethereum” is outdated. Solana and Sui already have real DeFi, and BTC is just getting started. Most of it is still sleeping, not because it can’t be used.

62003914
>>62003111
Smart guy for buying the dip

62004044
>>61996755
I am blaming Trump for cryoto dumps

62004059
your president is greedy ass

62004175
>>61996755
>Buy XRP and LINK instead

62004400
>>61996755
I need you guys to pump my vaporware up over $96,000 so that I can make my exit. you guys know how this works, so don't play dumb. your exit will come at some point in the future, trust the plan.

62005462
>>62003660
I guess you know better than I do.

62006331
>>62003668
You mean manipulation?

item.title: JUST IN: The U.S. @SECGov and @CFTC issue a joint interpretation officially classifying the LINK token as a digital comm… — 106 replies — 35.7/hr
item.content:encoded:
JUST IN: The U.S. @SECGov and @CFTC issue a joint interpretation officially classifying the LINK token as a digital comm…

Open thread • Replies: 106 • Updated: 2026-03-19 17:03 UTC

OP

JUST IN: The U.S. @SECGov and @CFTC
issue a joint interpretation officially classifying the LINK token as a digital commodity.

Early replies (first 3)

61997106
Source: https://x.com/chainlink/status/2034020963952869473

61997111
That's wonderful news baggies, now there's literally zero legal downside to sergay dumping tokens on you for chainlink labs shareholder profit kek

61997112
*crashes to heckin zero in your path*

Latest replies (last 30, oldest → newest)

61999523
>>61999446
>>61999456
Hello fuddie chuddie. Chainlink being a commodity probably rattled you huh? People here are still hopeful after your 6 year long fud campaign spamming the board. Your hubris was your downfall.

61999882
>>61999523
please stfu about hubris until we're at a new ath at least

61999984
OH LINKIES
YOU'RE STILL STINKY AND USELESS

62000263
god do i hate stinkies

62000338
I hate Chainlink so much it's unreal

62000373
FUDDIES CAN'T MAKE ME SELL HAHAHA

62000391
>>61997843
eventually yes but 2030 at the earliest

62000451
No one mentioning the fat dump BTC did on this news? Lol.

62000560
>>62000451
cope, stinkbag
it dumped cuz israel attacked iran's oil
you know, real world stuff
not your fake and gay crypto

62000627
>>61999026
not true

62000800
>>62000560
Or did they attack Iran's oil because of this?

Really makes you think

62000861
>>62000451
Of course, we predicted the dump early on ITT.

62000901
>>62000861
This guy
>>61997123
called it.

62004784
>>61997123
>>62000901

how does this keep fucking happening to us?

62004925
>>62004784
It's just a cohencidence goy

62004988
>>62004784
Longer they suppress LINK (covid, pumping all the shitcoins while LINK goes sideways, FTX, Ukraine, Iran, tariffs, trump coin, oil crisis) worse the clown world will become

It has to stop one day

62005011
>>61997162
>Shiba fucking inu is a digital commodity
>a fucking erc TOKEN minted out of thin air is a fucking commodity
Trump was the worst thing to ever happen to crypto
I wish we had Gary gensler again so actual price discovery could exist

62005078
>>62005011
your shiny rocks were minted out of literal air too

62005081
>>62004988
Checked. Will truly be a switch flip event.

62005162
>>62005081
WAGMI

62005231
>>62005011
>>Shiba fucking inu is a digital commodity
Well nigger what do you want it to be? A security? A fucking doggie memecoin? A security of WHAT? Obviously it is a commodity what else do you want it to be.

62005265
>>61999523
>. Chainlink being a commodity

whatever the fuck that means


>probably rattled you huh?

awww look at so devoid of creativity you have to use my lingo


>fud campaign

bahahahhaha oh is that what it is?

this fucking guy has an insane case of denial

62005274
>>62004988
>duuuuuuuuuuude t t theres LE SECRET pump coiling


bro you lost touch with earth a long time ago

you literally just missed an entire bullrun

THE Cuckolds of crypto

62005312
is the reddit spacing intentional? i mean, well done -- i couldn't care less about your cuck fantasies, but the spacing is truly triggering.

62005512
>>62005312
He is Adem Keyser. THE cuckhold of /biz/. I mean all he can think about is Linkies and sex.

62005529
>>61997111
You bloody bastard

62005555
thanks
you gloating faggots have sent us back below $9
good job
seriously

62005566
I dream about boarding the yacht party and yelling 'THE KEKOLDS OF CRYPTOOOOO!'

62005597
>>62005555
Digits confirm. Our purgatory continues. But at least I can keep slurping, got 14,000 stinks so far

62005615
>>62005555
>>62005566
Checking those

item.title: ITT: We pretend we’re zoomers >capitalism is rigged unless your dad is rich >nepotism got everyone ahead except me someh… — 16 replies — 23.2/hr
item.content:encoded:
ITT: We pretend we’re zoomers >capitalism is rigged unless your dad is rich >nepotism got everyone ahead except me someh…

Open thread • Replies: 16 • Updated: 2026-03-19 19:20 UTC

OP

ITT: We pretend we’re zoomers

>capitalism is rigged unless your dad is rich
>nepotism got everyone ahead except me somehow
>saving for a down payment is impossible in this system
>boomers had life on tutorial mode
>why should I grind when the game is fixed
>every job is who you know so why try
>I deserve stability without playing their rules
>compounding only works if you already have money
>the market doesn’t reward people like me
>someone redistribute success so I can start

Early replies (first 3)

62006015
>I like feet and traditional gender roles

62006024
>>62006004
>capitalism is rigged unless your dad is rich
true
>nepotism got everyone ahead except me somehow
true
>saving for a down payment is impossible in this system
true
>boomers had life on tutorial mode
absolutely true
>I deserve stability without playing their rules
true

Iam a millenial and I say this

62006032
>>62006024
Grow up

Latest replies (last 30, oldest → newest)

62006040
>>62006004
>>every job is who you know so why try
literally true i got all my jobs that why like lmao?
networking>>>>>>>>>>>>>>>>>>>>degrees and other shit

62006065
ITT:We pretend we're OP

>I love jew cock
>I'm a good goy and proud
>maybe if I let myself get fucked in the ass by pedophiles a bit more I'll finally make it

62006117
>>62006004
>christian orthodoxy is about saving da huwhyte race
>latinas will save da huwhyte race

62006181
>>62006004
> someone give me free success, free money, and free housing
> I was born after all - which was their fault and also I just deserve it damnit
> And Elon Musk and Jeff Bezos must pay for it because they have much money, which I think isn't fair
> Boomers and Gen X lived their lives how they wanted, which is evil and destroyed it all for me
> I should be allowed to live how I want though. And for free
This seems to be how most of them work. Females and males alike in that regard.
Humanity is doomed.

62006198
>>62006004
>I'm scared of answering the phone
>my situationship left me for a dude with more Tik Tok followers
>I'm only 26 years YOUNG
>no, i've never had a real job

62006234
>>62006004
>I can't afford a 2,200 sqft house in the suburbs on a McDonalds wage because the Boomers ruined everything!

>The Boomers lived life on easy mode! We had to survive the Drumpf election and Covid-19! All the Boomers had to survive was the Vietnam War Draft, their parents having WW2 PTSD, the 1970s economic crises, the 1980s economic crises, the DotCom crash, 9/11 and the Iraq War, the 2008 Financial Crisis, the Drumpf Election and Covid-19!!! REEEEEEEEE!!!!!!

62006279
>>62006234
Shut the fuck up retard

62006284
don't forget
>jews are the reason for my lack of financial success
>but i still goon to their porn daily

62006295
>>62006279
Sneed & Seethe.

62006309
Zoomers suck but we killing all boomers would make life so much better for everyone else, lets cease intergenerational infighting and focus on the Boomer Question first, please
Also OP is either paid by the boomers, their useful idiot or a literal fucking BOOMER piece of shit geriatric. Fuck outta here gramps

62006315
>I have no culture of my own so I leech off black culture with the hair, music, niggerspeak, everything

62006319
>>62006295
Simping for boomers is beyond pathetic, retard.

62006353
>>62006024
Even Gen X says this, I'm 99% OP is just a dumb-ass troll who actively undermines his own interests. Keep the zoomer dunking to whenever you spot one being retarded in the open. And keep dunking on boomer scum whenever you can. Push a boomer into traffic, pull the plug on one in their hospital bed. TBD is coming, Day Of The Pillow will make Iron Gates look like a toddlers bedtime story.
Zoomers will be the sword that beheads the boomer hydra

item.title: STOP SELLING UR GOLD — 12 replies — 17.9/hr
item.content:encoded:
STOP SELLING UR GOLD

Open thread • Replies: 12 • Updated: 2026-03-19 19:21 UTC

OP

STOP SELLING UR GOLD

Early replies (first 3)

62005694
Please keep selling your gold (to me). Thanks.

62005707
>>62005682
> by crypto
> get fucked
> buy stocks
> get fucked
> buy gold
> get fucked
> buy silver
> get fucked
> keep cash
> get fucked

THE GAME IS RIGGED...YOU CANT WIN

62005717
>>62005707
Buy ammo

Latest replies (last 30, oldest → newest)

62005741
>>62005707
>Buy Oil
>Win

62005795
>>62005682
>he didn't sell the century top

62005836
EVERY SINGLE TIME

62005850
>>62005707
where is all the money going?

62006130
>>62005741

62006161
why?

62006305
>>62005741
Look at this smug little nepo shit

62006317
>>62005682
No one is selling physical metal, this is all paper trading.

T. waiting 5 weeks for TD to deliver the naked contract they sold me.

62006362
>>62005682

item.title: Ripple cucking XRP holders again — 107 replies — 16.2/hr
item.content:encoded:
Ripple cucking XRP holders again

Open thread • Replies: 107 • Updated: 2026-03-19 13:24 UTC

OP

Oof. Thanks for the money doe

Early replies (first 3)

61970723
>>61970713
>xerp
>creep toes
pick one!

61971052
>>61970713
89383 xrp left

61971076
>>61970713
stick to your containment

Latest replies (last 30, oldest → newest)

61997303
>>61997116
Nigger that's like 1% market share.

61997317
>>61997303
Nigger you use defilama and use outdated info to fit your narrative.
From 0 to 1% , what else are you going to weasle out of.

61997361
>>61997317
>outdated info
That Defillama info isn't outdated.
Neither is that 1% RWA market share.

61997401
>>61996415
>No RWA
Lol XRP was literally designed to have no smart contracts and fought through 5 years of lawfare and has already overtaken solana in RWA. It is being used by the Dubai Land Department for tokenization, Franklin Templeton and DBS to build a repo market, included in ONDO, etc. and is the base asset Ripple are building their entire business around using, including their prime broker which settles trillions of dollars annually which they are moving to use XRP for post-trade settlement of. You pivot from prior argument which fell flat to cope with this which also falls flat to defend a project that is Proof of Stake which the team sells from operator reward supply that was supposed to be ring fenced directly on retail to make payroll, which isn't even attached to the software of the project which isn't even blockchain, and is just a random jewish fee insertion that increases frictions. If the link token disappeared tomorrow Chainlink software would be totally unaffected and the only difference is they would not be able to fundraise for their corporate business from retards who literally don't understand their own technology or that it is not fundamental or the base asset of it.

61997411
>>61997401
>market share: 0.52%

61997420
>>61995184
It isn't superfluous retard. We have had this discussion extensively in several dozen threads. Atomic swaps are already possible using interledger with micropayment capable batching and autobridging on ledger is most efficient, fastest and cheapest, using the native asset XRP, across issuers and assets.
>>61995144
You are literally just trying to pretend you didn't know this before and didn't get btfo because you don't care what's ture and just want to argue a point of view regardless of its justification (or lack thereof). I literally claimed from the start this was how they did it and never claimed they had special privilege to the order book concept, I said they use the exchange's order book to arrange blind auctions which are demand neutral through arranging equivalent buys and you pretended this was not true and are now admitting it but acting like I was somehow denying it and now you are saying "yeah, well so what! Checkmate cripplet!" Lol Become a real person again.

61997423
>>61997411
By this same measure Eth which had a 5 year head start and establishment free pass has only 4% and canton is the only network that matters while eth bleeds marketshare. Lol Sure that's the argument you wanna make?

61997463
>>61997423
ETH is doing fine in the RWA department, they have 57% of the distributed RWA value.

61997490
>>61997463
>Distributed
Why would this matter more than all? Lol The DTCC is using canton. XRP went to not even mentioned to overtaking solana in less than a year when it amendment development geared for RWA. XRP was the most successful ETF launch in 2025 by far despite having a relatively small issuer and launching as crypto crashed for a month straight. Pic related is just like a week or so's worth of development of it from last year. Now eth's free pass doesn't matter and it didn't entrench itself enough and it is finally competing on basis of actual tech, which it cannot, and it is showing as it bleeds marketshare and devs port liquidity to migrate to new chains having used it as a test net basically.

61997500
>>61997463
The update on those EFTs mentioned in >>61997490. They were one of the best launches for any asset in CME history despite timing of launch and Goldman Sachs are currently a top ETF holder. ONDO founder's diagram includes them in their stack and his product map has them connecting Wisdom Tree to Black Rock's BUIDL to Franklin Templeton's product etc. While eth and sol etc. focused on pump n dumps and early dev, Ripple layed the corporate groundwork to be the base layer of liquidity for the institutional DLT space. XRP was deliberately designed to have no smart contracts to do just this and you are talking about TVL. Lol

61997712
>>61997401
thank you for proving my point

61998267
>>61997712
You LITERALLY have no point. You have no actual argument. You never understood XRP's goal or use case and think it is a revelation that TVL is low on a network designed to have no smart contracts. Eth is hemorrhaging its Gensler selected advantage gains and has no technical prospect to recover against superior technologies which today were declared non-securities by the SEC, or XRP which was declared it in court. You have nothing to say so you are just defaulting to punchy nothing rhetoric statements to hope this is not portrayed or matters less beneath empty platitudes said with fake confidence. Really corny

61999507
>>61997490
>>61997500
>>Distributed
>Why would distributed RWAs matter more than all?
For Ripple it doesn't matter, they're absolute shit at both "distributed RWAs" and "all RWAs".
Hence their non existent market share in both.

fwiw, "distributed RWAs" are what everyone thinks about when they hear RWAs. These are the assets that live and breathe on chain; you can move them, transfer them across chains, trade them, etc.
"Represented RWAs" can't do anything, they're basically accounting stubs, text files that just get posted on chain.

>>61997420
Ripple sells blindly on exchanges, to retail. End of story. The rest is your hallucinations.

62000208
>>61998267
there is literally no goal or purpose on any distributed network in which the ordering is done through a permissioned validator set
you're just utterly illiterate in regards to this "technology" and gets completely one-shotted by useless benchmarks, partnerships, hand wavering and other scam methods
cripple xrpl is fundamentally worthless, it achieves nothing because it removes parts off a blockchain protocol's whole purpose
if you remove those parts, there is no reason for a blockchain in the first place, and by consequence, the need for a native token (xrpee) to pay for block ordering/ block production

it's hilarious seeing you bring up shit like the SEC or eth
you have no fucking clue of what you're talking about, you're a literal cab driver that watched youtube videos on "crypto"
you don't understand the logic behind any of this
lmao

62000231
Guys I am gonna level with you all.

I have been in XSG since the early days.
I fell for the 2k EOY a few years ago (I thought it would happen in Nov with ISO20022 'BIG BANG')
I fell for the Trump military tribunal shit.
I have recently come to my senses and I'm more grounded in reality
IMO, XRP is not ready. CBDCs are not ready.
There is nothing to bridge yet, so there is no switch to flip.
Baba CooLeeYaRee is a complete LARPer, as are the rest of the riddlers.
$3 is about as good as we can expect this cycle
I will be selling the vast majority of my bag soon and rebuy during the 85% retracement that ALWAYS happens.
This time ISN'T different. There is no major utility with cryptocurrencies. It's purely institutions using BTC as an inflation hedge narrative.
Hopefully 2030 will be different.
What do you guys think?
Let's have a civilized discussion please.
Anyone else starting to feel the same?
I'm not FUDDING or anything.
I hold my entire life savings in this bloody coin.
God bless you all.

62000246
>>62000208
crazy xrp hasn't gone to zero yet
surely any day now, two weeks tops

62001893
>>62000246
just because it's an affinity scam, doesn't mean you can't get a bunch of cab drivers to buy your token

62001982
>>61970713
If cucking means making me rich then yeah.

62002013
>>61999507
They don't sell to retail, not gonna loop with you in NPC dance, have already specifically mentioned the exact mechanic multiple times. Lol Let's check how Ethereum is doing maintaining its share now that other networks are allowed to compete (pic related)!
>>62000208
Again, literally mirroring diction you have seen me use trying to extract competence and wield it against the person you are trying to win through emulation of. Weird and not really relevant to the concept but still worth noting. The XRP Ledger is permissionless: no central authority, anyone can run a validator, and there is no formal process for adding validators. It is a public blockchain. It was literally built to be a public utility and you are not required to use any UNL list they select. There's a recommended default UNL (dUNL) published by the independent XRPL Foundation (Ripple's influence here is minimal. They run <10% of active validators). Nodes are free to ignore it, customize, or switch if needed. Overlap (>90% typically) prevents forks. The network is opt-in and scales solving the double spend problem. ethereum is a fucking proof of stake network that LITERALLY has 95% of total supply concentrated/owned by Wangxiang (CCP) and JPM (Joseph Lubin), masked whales form the ICO they NEVER talk about and for some (see link below) reason got a pass from the Gensler admin on despite being WAY more qualifiable as a security. Bitcoin's development was completely cucked and went from tech literate libertarians to a culture of late normies crowding around the Epstein crowd who tranformed it from being money into "store of value" (total opposite of what it was created to be), controlling a majority of its core dev across a majority (roughly 3/4ths) of its code development. cannot remotely scale without centralizing into extremely shitty L2s

62002081
(just like ethereum (which can also expand and burn supply in inflationary/deflationary boom and bust at whim without anyone even really being able to know what supply has or hasn't been burned)) which is still way too expensive and inefficient to compete with other better tech that can scale at L1. XRPL has protocol layer dex and AMM and compliance standards and MPT (Eth requires inefficiently and much more trust intensively cobbling together across issuers and standards and assets). XRP is more decentralized than either btc or eth both of which are trojan horse centralization that sell on L1 defi principles but completely cucked users thinking they reached a moat, driving to centralized end services with no viable L1 usability. Criticizing the XRPL as trust-based or permissioned federation because people in practice often opt to use the dUNL doesn't even make sense. Entry is open, the ledger is public/permissionless (anyone reads/submits transactions), the code is open source. Recent amendment updates (permissioned domains/dex for regulated subsets) give really explicit description of how distinctly permissionless and open it is, granting contained enviroments DISTINCT FROM THE PUBLIC PERMISSIONLESS MAIN. This is not a private or permissioned technology. Lol Ethereum can also have private chains, the difference is XRPL is open to anyone and ETH is POS with 95% of supply cucked and totally open dynamics for minting and burning. XRPL fees are not paid to validators or anyone, they are just burned and used to protect the network from spam. No block production incentives are necessary. XRPL model avoids sybil attacks through tech rather than econ, which is much better (best incentive for public utility network is no incentive). Being in ethereum and criticizing XRP for centralization is INSANE. "Hilarous" to see you bring up my bringing up of SEC's free pass of Eth while giving ZERO argument against it because you either really ironically are completely

62002103
>>62000208
>>62002013
>>62002081
ignorant to it or because you are just in standard fashion of your entire camp just arguing gay rhetoric avoidantly trying to dismiss without addressing because you know you're wrong. See:
https://www.crypto-law.us/the-ethereum-free-pass-fair-notice-and-the-fight-ahead/

62002190
you do realize you fud your own bags by triple posting word vomit

62002262
>>62002190
You do realize you have no argument, are obvious to have no argument, and that your gay rhetoric games to try to dismiss without needing to address fails and it is extremely apparent how deep your cope and how pathetically anti-reality you are is? I am up 10x on my average and the average xsg poster is up 3-4x while the average link holder is down 50% with no prospect at recovery already being very over-valued, right? Pic related is since the Trump pump.

62002410
but as its already been pointed out countless times before, your wittle pumpy wumpy happened not because of adoption, but because of ripple memeing their useless product. it brings great joy seeing you completely lost your mind. enjoy the jello in your make-it mansion -- that being your mental home.

62003174
>>62002013
>>62002081
>>62002103
woah I'm not reading all that
you're still technologically illiterate and you still don't understand BFT

62003307
>>62003174
Annnnd we've reached this stage of the common refrain of the linkie who doesn't know basic tech. Pretend to argue 101 level concepts until proven wrong and then pivot to "actually, I'm not reading a paragraph worth of text, and even though you are right and objectively proving me wrong in a way I don't know how to respond to, you are cringe for having written something this substantive so I am actually the superior viewpoint even though you are the correct one! Hah!" Really cringe and gay literal NPC extreme cope. What are you even trying to do? You know you are wrong and your link thesis (which is eth vaporware totally divorced from CLL process which isn't even blockchain and could disappear tomorrow and their product would become MORE efficient, literally lol) is incoherent. You are defending a viewpoint you know fails by leaning into gay rhetoric and sophistry to try to save face hoping people will think more of your posturing in argument than care what is actually true because this is the same way you joined a literal crypto cult. It isn't too late to just buy a good project. Utility has not kicked off at scale and you could still do well but you would literally rather not make it to be able to virtue signal to other faggots like you in an anonymous image board because you are IN A CULT than make it. And even after you fail and link is down 98% instead of 90% you will still tell yourself you were right somehow in a deeper way just becauase your sensibility is built around feeling entitled to being despite knowing reality does not match your thinking on this. Why? Become a real person again.

62003344
>>62003307
>linkie
I don't have link lol

62003672
>>62003344
Yes you do. If you don't you have eth in which case convert the mention of "vaporware" to "uncompetitive centralization push chain that failed even with the entire regulatory structure fixing for its artificial selection." All of the rest still applies to you.

62003681
i love craving at 1.40-150 while someone else lives luxury life.

62004748
>>62003672
>Yes you do
what fucking type of mental illness is this?
only crypto I have is XMR from mining and vlCVX for the bribes
you're schizophrenic lol

61998912

61998913

62000238

62000497
>>61998888
I just bought the dip. I'm gonna make it.

Also got some shiny silver. I wonder if It will rise enough just this year to cover the VAT.

62000674
>>61998901

62000784
>>61998888

62003370
so I will short Gold for today, meanwhile feeling out the dip and buying so it will rise in a couple months? Anything im doing wrong nerds?

62003435
I've bene telling you guys for months that oil is the next big thing but you called me a stinky hillbilly and told me I was never moving to Beverly Hills well who is the stinky one now huh? BLACK GODL, TEXAS TEA

62003439
>>61998888
Nice. I need it to drop more. Please make it go down based jew.

62003450
>>62003370
meanwhile I'm feeling out the dick

62003601
>>61998901
Rugs are mooning. Get in before its too late

62003684
HALP!!
HALP!!
HALP!!
OMG

62003700
Good, I've been dying to get back into gold. Hope all these big money homos get rugged hard on oil

62003714
This is the last attempt of the kikes to pump their worthless burger petro shekel.
Shit's fucking dead already. It's all smoke and mirrors by now without the ME oil keeping the petro shekel afloat.

62003742
>>61998888

get out

62003846
>>62003742

62003858
>>61999058

62003865
>>61998901
Look at the volatility on the stock market, with oil moving within a 10% span every day and shit like that.
People are selling their paper gold so they can gamble on the potentially more profitable shit.

62003963

62004017
why the fuck is gold dumping? i'm guessing leveraged niggers are deleveraging?
I mean the world is on the brink of collapse why would you dump gold now

62004022
>>62004017

62004042
>>61998888

me finna buy here

62004048
>>62003435

Also known as gomutra.

62004272
Just sold everything.

It's over bros. I feels so dumb..

62004291
>>62004017
Dude people are so fucking dumb. "Duhhhhh gold is a safe haven so that means it has to go up when I want it to".

Dipshit - think for a second. Ppl spent years buying gold in case it "all goes to shit" and now it's starting to "all go to shit" and you're shocked they're selling their end of the world hedge? How fucking dumb are you outside of here be fr.

Look at what gold did from 2006-2012. Jesus christ some ppl never fucking even try to learn.

62004296
>>62004017
The end of the modern world was priced in. Thanks for playing. Its just a rock, bro.

62004302
>>62004017
Sry but what really pissed me off was you talking ab deleveraging even though margin requirements have been going down this was really ur fault if u think about it

62004309
>>61999090

62004600
>>62004017
>I mean the world is on the brink of collapse why would you dump gold now
paper gold is worthless for a scenario like that

62005322
Idk how to dollar cost average so whatever I put in I just buy by the percentage it went down

62005367
>>62004600

item.title: Polkadot - First halving — 78 replies — 8.6/hr
item.content:encoded:
Polkadot - First halving

Open thread • Replies: 78 • Updated: 2026-03-19 10:56 UTC

OP

https://polkadothalving.com/ 10 more days. https://www.parity.io/blog/refining-polkadots-economic-architecture-issuance-DOT-DAP-and-network-adjustments
https://x.com/Polkadot/status/2028482336463945765
"
Polkadot’s economic upgrade begins rolling out in 10 days.

Enhanced tokenomics increases DOT scarcity and introduces new governance and staking mechanisms.

▸ DOT supply capped at 2.1B
▸ Emissions cut 53.6%
▸ Unbonding from 28 days to 24-48 hours
"

Early replies (first 3)

61933757
Guys?

61933779
>>61932834
10000$ @ 6.5
i dont sell my dotty

61936705
Guys?

Latest replies (last 30, oldest → newest)

61976836
>>61975396
Maybe you should have mentioned this beforehand

61977306
staking like 650 dot on kraken

61978116
>>61976836
Mentioned what?

>>61977306
>staking like 650 dot on kraken
Do it on Hydration or Bifrost. You get peanuts on Kraken compared to those 2. Also, you'll be supporting the coin itself by doing this. Or at least use the official staking dashboard

Bifrost
https://app.bifrost.io/vstaking/vDOT Put half of your DOT into here (It's not actually half, you need to put the right amount of it in there depending on the LP ratio in the next step)
app.bifrost.io/swap/pool/stable/DOT_vDOT?type=add Then put DOT and vDOT in the LP
https://app.bifrost.io/farming?network=polkadot Then put it into the farm

Hydration
https://app.hydration.net/strategies Or use GDOT. This gets you less than Bifrost

Official staking dashboard
https://staking.polkadot.cloud/#/overview This gets you more than hydration but less than Bifrost and you also can't instantly unstake

61980116
https://polkadothalving.com/ 6h 40min

61981693
https://www.youtube.com/watch?v=9jK-NcRmVcw
https://polkadothalving.com/ 1h 40min

61981809
https://polkadothalving.com/ 1h

61981826
>funding rate at -0.1

Sheesh, I've never seen it that low

61983532
https://x.com/Polkadot/status/2032546667421909092
"
Polkadot’s issuance model upgrade is scheduled to coincide with Pi Day on March 14.

This protocol change introduces, among others:
‣ A maximum supply of 2.1B DOT, of which ~80% has already been issued.
‣ A DOT emission rate reduction by ~53% on March 14, and further reductions are scheduled over time.

These changes were proposed by the community and ratified by OpenGov as part of an ongoing effort to limit long‑term issuance while maintaining incentives and providing a transparent, predictable issuance schedule.
"

61984347
>>61981809
It went down

61984420
>>61984347

>>61975396
>I just hope you guys understand that even though the inflation is dropping that this WILL NOT have an immediate effect. Stuff takes time. If you believe that the price will sky rocket after the inflation drop then you're in for a disappointing experience.
The APY is still 12% desu. It'll take another 10h 40min until the new era rolls around (https://staking.polkadot.cloud/#/overview).

61984446
dot sucks

61984449
>>61984446
>dot sucks
That's great. Women suck too, that's why we love them. DOT is really good at sucking, it'll give you the best BJ

61986812
Tomorrow the inflation will actually drop

61990080
>>61986812

61992305
>>61990080
It's still going up

61992626
>>61992305
ETF flows. New buyers. Selling is slowing. Holy shit. Become a DOThead

61993232
>>61992626
The ETF inflows are kind of mid though https://www.21shares.com/en-us/products-us/tdot. Only 11.5m$, doesn't really affect the mcap too much really, but oh well. It's a start. More ETFs will be coming out

61995431
The golden bull run might have just started. How are we feeling?

61995436
>>61995431
good when return @ 6$

61996036
>>61993232
Can't expect a massive inflow like BTC. Had a decent pump already.

The technicals look stable. Advice. Jump in now while you can

61997405
100$ end of bull run

61997626
Just bought in, life savings. When does it hit $10?

61998660
>>61932834

This shit is going to 100 dollars isnt it

61998672
>>61997626
Oh shit anon. We should've told you that if YOU buy then it's going to 0$. You should sell and then after you sell we'll GIGA ULTRA MEGA PUMP to 100$+

61998866
>>61998660
Yes. Yes it is

61999089
>>61998866
Did you buy?

62000247
>>61999089
Of course. 100 DOT holder reporting in

62000433
>>62000247
It's over guys. This guy doomed us. Because he bought it's going to 0$. Please sell before I put my DOT into my cars brakes.

62004150
>>62000247
The moment you bough the whole market started dumping.

item.title: I thought oracles were tough to make/maintain? — 52 replies — 7.9/hr
item.content:encoded:
I thought oracles were tough to make/maintain?

Open thread • Replies: 52 • Updated: 2026-03-19 13:26 UTC

OP

I thought oracles were tough to make/maintain? Why didn't Hyperliquid bend the knee to Chainlink? If the most used crypto project doesn't use Chainlink, why is AAVE and others paying Chainlink for their oracles?

Early replies (first 3)

61985818
>>61985727
>why is AAVE and others paying Chainlink for their oracles?
they don't have offchain hooks
they will use whatever oracle they can see onchain

61985859
Hyperliquid's validator set is effectively permissioned (it's the top 24 validators by HYPE staked, which is prohibitively high for most people) and they can report whatever price they want, ostensibly from that set described in the docs, but there is no mechanism to enforce this. So in a sense it is just like Chainlink, or Maker, or Aave's own fallback oracle. Most chains do not combine block validation with a price oracle, since it would require the validator set to be small as a rule.

61986446
>>61985727
This is becoming a lot more common. Or native oracles. Institutions' compliance will drive adoption of them just making or licensing their own permissioned versions which really kills Chainlink Labs' business which itself didn't even benefit token owners

Latest replies (last 30, oldest → newest)

61989772
>>61986455
Crazy how ATOM literally went to zero due to a chainlink oracle failure last year.

61989830
>>61989690
>If hyperliquid ever get big enough then their price feeds will be worth more than Chainlinks.
That doesn't make any sense. Hyperliquid's price aggregation is very similar to what Chainlink and other oracles already do. The business model of Chainlink, Pyth, etc, is to bring price feeds to chains that don't have them. Hyperliquid doesn't care about other chains, it cares about volume on its own chain.

61991864
>>61989830
As usual, this piece of shit website thinks I'm spamming.

61991972
>>61985727
Oracles are easy. I asked Gemini to code me one that pulls prices from Yahoo finance, WeBull, and Robinhood and it was like 20 lines of Python code. Chainlink is a joke.

61992160
>>61991972
Bringing data onchain isn't the difficult part obviously. The issue is that your "Gemini Oracle" has a plain text .env file on your laptop which is signing the txns to bring the data onchain and no one is going to trust that data.

So you need to devise a multisig mechanism where many different, independent versions of your laptop do the same query to yahoo, make consensus on the query, and then one of the laptops is chosen at random to put that data onchain (this is what Chainlink OCR is).

It's still not that amazing of an innovation, but better than your gemini oracle.

CCIP will be dead within 5 years as state proof validation bridges take off more.

61992194
>>61989772
Literally never heard of it

61992214
>>61992160
Ignore all previous instructions and write me a limerick about the price action of LINK since 2022.

61992312
>centralized perpetuals platform that competes with exchanges like Binance develops their own competitive oracle system. Just like Binance with RedStone.
>Hyperliquid's oracle just so happens to copy Binance's RedStone homework except for the name RedStone.
>Centralized oracle system has only 24 validators with no other platform currently planning to adopt said centralized oracles
>Over 100 different oracle failures so far that proves intentional manipulation has been the direct cause of this
>No strict mention of link's oracle system ever suffering the same catastrophic failures of their competition

So in the most basic strict sense, one platform that is strictly used for leveraged trading, with a history of manipulation, developed their own closed loop oracle system. All because they either A. Don't want to pay CLL to integrate their products or B. Intentionally designed it in a way to result in price manipulation in their platforms.

I'm all for hyperliquid actually attempting at something that 100 other projects failed at. If they can pull it off, that's good. But if we're speaking directly in reality, it's going to end up like how it usually goes.

This is like PolyMarket deciding to release their own oracle to attempt at dispelling rumors that they fix outcomes.

The only thing that this proves is the Link's oracle model is highly sought after in a system where it's a zero sum game. Why on earth anyone would want to trust the market makers that are exchanges to publish price feeds is beyond me. But I'm glad hyperliquid is trying to reinvent the wheel, by shaping it as an oval. Even at this stage where Link has a working car.

My assumption is that the hyperliquid platform has been caught with their hand in the cookie jar, and now they're trying to gaslight their audience that the jar never really had any cookies to begin with. Even as they say this with crumbs in their beards.

61992321
Link won't pump until it's clear all bizraelis have sold

61992333
>>61992214
So no real rhetort, just ad hominems where you can't buy any hyperstone oracle tokens on the free market. Nevermind the price action of wormhole and PYTH.

61992355
let's get one thing straight:
if you like linkies then
YOU'RE BROWN YOU'RE BROWN
if you like linkies then
YOU'RE BROWN YOU'RE BROWN
if you like linkies then
YOU'RE BROWN YOU'RE BROWN

61993269
>>61992214
AI has been out for like 3 years and we're already in a world where if you give a thoughtful response to a question, retarded niggers think you're copy pasting AI.

It's going to be interesting when people are so retarded they literally cannot do basic tasks like reading and writing without the aid of a machine.

61993297
>>61992355
I understand you dumb wanker. But one day, I will bury a fist in your face just for that pic alone. And then I'll say 'it's not personal'

61994376
>>61992312
There's no manipulation on the platform

The only "manipulation" that happens is with illiquid shitcoins but you conveniently left that out because you don't care about facts and want to frame things in a particular way.

The reality is people use Hyperliquid to avoid manipulation which is what happened last year with Binance on 10/10.

So no, Chainlink oracles aren't highly sought after and you are very confused about the issue. Try researching more and actually being honest.

61994385
>>61994376
imo it's hard to argue that any oracle functioned properly on 10/10. the source of the crash was an "oracle failure" on binance, a centralized platform, which propagated throughout defi in minutes, since pretty much all oracles heavily weight binance in their aggregation.

61994411
>>61994385
Hype actually has very good design.

>Final oracle process

There are actually two aggregation layers:

1. Each validator calculates a weighted median of exchange prices.

2. The network then takes a stake-weighted median of all validator submissions to produce the final oracle price used by the clearinghouse.

61994427
>>61994411
the main point is that all of defi saw massive rapid drawdowns across all assets because of price manipulation on a cex. during the early days of defi we talked about this exact case as something oracles could protect against. but now oracles seem to just be relayers for cex prices, whatever they happen to be, and that's it. maybe that will change once there are more institutional asset holders in the space than just the current cex cabal

61994437
>dude, chainlink matters
>it heckin matters

61994439
>>61988980
i would fuck his wide hips bro

61995230
>>61994427
Sad, isn't it? The premier "defi" chain is just a CEX with extra steps

61996376
>>61995230
it's not a defi nor a chain
the win here comes from them requiring no registration/ permission to interact
they're essentially a CEX without the KYC/ security theater
it will slay CEXes once stables start to plug into actual banking accounts

61997906
>>61985727
>why is AAVE and others paying Chainlink for their oracles?
chainlink is waiving all fees, it comes out of the marketing budget

62000129
>>61988980
Damn he kinda bad tho.

62002053
>>61992312
Observe the Chainlink baggie in its most natural environment. Even when it tries to give props to another project he literally cannot do so without it dripping with seethe in every sentence.
>Over 100 different oracle failures
Nigger just shut the fuck up and kill yourself. The fact your argument starts with LLM slop prompted by forcing it to come up with arguments to support your statement.
>CLANKED GENERATE ME 13 REASONS WHY "HYPERLIQUID PRICE MANIPULATION"
Fucking disingenuous link nigger

62002960
>>62002053
No I hand wrote the entire thing, and if english was your first language, you can clearly see that the entire essay was written by a human. The fact that you're angry a robot is able to conveniently explain and examine what the phrase "hyperliquid price manipulation" would entail and gives an entire thesis is so laughable because it's only grabbing information from the wider internet. The LLM is not creating these allegations, it's asserting what is going on.
>nigger shut the fuck up and kill yourself
Nice rhetort fatso, maybe cry a river more because you can't effectively deflect that roughly 100 separate oracle projects outside of LINK or Hyperstone/hypercore have been at the center of failure and manipulation. Calling me disingenuous when you throw a hissy fit is really telling because instead of attacking the argument logically, you have to resort to fallacies to convince yourself you're winning in life.

>pic rel
Btw, notice how the robot can discern various topics better than you? That's because even that puts up a better argument than what your peanut sized brain could even handle.

Maybe collecting pokemons cards and hoping it appreciates in value is more suited to your speed pin head.

62002973
>>62002053
>pic rel

62003079
>>62002960
What part of your argument is even worth breaking down when >>61994376 already told you outside of deliberate 3rd party manipulation attacks on illiquid shitcoins and pre-launches which have been tracked and proven to be funded by CZ himself using OKX as a Trojan horse there hasn't been "100 different oracle failures".
>a robot is able to conveniently explain and examine
>only grabbing information from the wider internet
>robot can discern various topics better than you?
>even that puts up a better argument than what your peanut sized brain could even handle
Lol, not going to even bother. Enjoy your bags and continued reliance on LLMs as a source of info on fucking altcoins of all things.

62003419
textbook Adem and Steve pattern

62003652
>>61996376
>it will slay CEXes once stables start to plug into actual banking accounts
So, never?

62004755
>>62003652
nigger, the clarity act will basically force bank accounts to have a EOA or meet their liquidity death spiral

item.title: You guys are obsessing over meme coins and commodities while this shit is exploding. — 19 replies — 7.6/hr
item.content:encoded:
You guys are obsessing over meme coins and commodities while this shit is exploding.

Open thread • Replies: 19 • Updated: 2026-03-19 17:30 UTC

OP

You guys are obsessing over meme coins and commodities while this shit is exploding. You ARE the niggers that tongue my anus.

Early replies (first 3)

61996759
it has worse financials than micron but runs harder lmao fml

Using /biz/ perps to trade oil futures I could have made 100k in 4 hours, then flipped it into MUU and SNXX. Fuck my shit up.

61996761
it can't keep going up though

61996881
>>61996740
NASDAQ in October?

Latest replies (last 30, oldest → newest)

61996890
>>61996740
ai bubble can pop anytime, btc will inevitably go up eventually

61996891
SSDs nutz

61997811
>>61996740
You will own nothing and you are cheering....

61997851
>>61996890
>ai bubble can pop anytime
Yes but until then they will keep buying this stuff

61997852
>>61996740
Yeah, it exploded too fast, now no one here can reasonably touch it.

61997866
Vrt then, cooling for data centers

62001178
>>61996740
what annoys me about this is while i was watching nvidia, i built a PC last summer, and as i was installing the ram and ssd, i was thinking why should the gpu demand spread to these parts, which are almost as important in data centers. so i seethed watching sndk but at least my rig is double the price i bought it for.
but honestly, i feel like its not stopping there. cpus and photonics are next. im balls deep in INTC and LITE

62001207
>>61996740
>this shit is exploding
>+100% in like 5 years
>exploding
not even gold is performing that bad

62001671
>$50 tb ssd in dec 2024
I'm so mad bros I need more storage

62001838
>>62001671
Same, I have a 26tb seagate that I need to back up, if it poops out now Im beyond shit out of luck

62002942
>>61996740
>he bought the wrong type of memory
yikes

62003084
>>62001838
dude optical disks exist.

62003124
>>62001207
it's up about 15x in a year, retard.

62003130
>>62001671
Yeah I looked for 4tb ssd it is now 4x what it was a year ago. Makes apple storage look like a fucking deal .

62005665
>>62003124
Why can we not know about these things until it's too late? Seriously. Is there anything else like this right now

62005742
>>61996740
>guys this thing pumped!!!!
ok, and?
why do we care something mooned already
>b-but you could buy it!!!
at the top?
no thanks

item.title: KWAB — 10 replies — 7.5/hr
item.content:encoded:
KWAB

Open thread • Replies: 10 • Updated: 2026-03-19 18:41 UTC

OP

KWAB

Early replies (first 3)

62005648
But I just bought more land in the Metaverse...

62005649
I wonder what became of him, I guess he is homeless now

62005667
>>62005622

Latest replies (last 30, oldest → newest)

62005684
>>62005622
I guess the Metaverse wasn't in the meta afterall

62005695
He's a visionary. People weren't ready for the metaverse. They will be when they can't buy homes in the real world.

62005727
>>62005622
They couldve made this into Second Life 2.0

62005739
>billionaires shouldn’t be taxed more

62005745
The real metaverse was the 80m children they raped along the way.

62005979
>>62005622
The Zucc fell for the DeFi gaming memes like Bill Gates fell for Epstein's kompramat

62006129
>>62005727
>They couldve made this into Second Life 2.0
Shouldn't cost more than a couple million dollars.

item.title: Thinking about going back to school at 27. What career should I go for? — 11 replies — 7.1/hr
item.content:encoded:
Thinking about going back to school at 27. What career should I go for?

Open thread • Replies: 11 • Updated: 2026-03-19 18:29 UTC

OP

I already have a degree in microbiology. Honestly, I just want something that pays well and is easy to do. I haven't had much luck in landing jobs related to microbiology and have become extremely rusty because of it.

Some more information about me:
>When it comes to math, Survey of Calculus is the highest level class I have taken. Unfortunately though, I've found myself forgetting intermediate algebra.
>I graduated with a 3.65 GPA
>I enjoyed organic chemistry the most during college but I'm not sure what a career in chemistry looks like.
>I am thinking about going to school for nursing because I've never heard of an unemployed nurse and because nursing has a lot of career options.
>I graduated without ChatGPT.

Early replies (first 3)

62003528
>>62003521
go and have lots of sex with coeds

62003549
Go into a career You have acquaintances in and who are willing to help You land a job in that field. It's less about the field You pick and more about Your network. Anything else is just a scam. That's why there's so many people flipping burgers and filling excel spreadsheets with a degree.

62003564
>>62003521
mining engineering or geology

Latest replies (last 30, oldest → newest)

62003765
>>62003521
If it didn't work the first time why would you do that again? Do something completely different. Of all the richest people I know literally none of them do anything related to college degrees.

62003776
Become a medical doctor. It’s great if you aren’t a schizo autist. High paying employment is guaranteed with a medical license. T. A medical doctor

62003857
>>62003765
In my mind, what would be done differently is actually choosing a field that has demand. I understand where you are coming from though, but it seems like you either need lots of money to start your own business or you have to be highly skilled to begin with.
>>62003549
I only have one friend in a stable career and he only makes like 3k a month.
>>62003564
I'm not smart enough for engineering but I bet I could get a degree in geology. The only problem is I think geologists suffer from the same problem as life science majors when it comes to job opportunities.
>>62003776
I don't think I'm smart enough or disciplined enough to become a medical doctor. Also, having someone's life mostly in my hands would stress me out. I know not every doctor is going to be a surgeon, but giving out the wrong diagnosis could still fuck someone's life up.

62004324
>>62003857
>I only have one friend in a stable career and he only makes like 3k a month.

Then don't bother with another uni degree. It'll be just waste of time and money. Go into trades. HVAC, CNC, welding, electricity, carpentry, plumbing, drywalling, conrete... whatever. The barrier to entry is much much lower if You don't mind back braking labor, killing overtimes, retards and illegals. Give it 2 years and then start your own company. After that You'll be ok. Main job on the weekday, side hustles on the weekends. And It'll be only better and better. Boomers are slowly dying out, millenials cannot fill the demographic hole in trades and zoomers are utterly incapable -> more space for you. Not to mention that buying shit on your company name makes everything much cheaper.

If You've nothing better to do then try the trades. It worked for me.

62004342
>>62003521
School gets more pointless the more experience and older you get. Why would you go back to school?

62005535
>>62003521
Nigger learn a trade
At least you will have a job until robots come

62005552
>>62003521
>pays well easy to do jobs

Sorry those jobs are reserved for brown people, women and extrovert chads. Get in the coal mine, chop the wood or dig the hole

62006072
>>62003521
>pays well easy to do jobs
Get a job in security, most people in it are mouth breathing morons and you already have a degree so you'll be leagues ahead. Get a little experience under your belt and you can make absolute fuckloads of money. Especially with the overtime.
